Title: The Vacuolar Inositol Transporter BvINT1;1 Contributes to Raffinose Biosynthesis and Reactive Oxygen Species Scavenging During Cold Stress in Sugar Beet

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: EMS-treated multigerm sugar beet population; segregating sister lines (WT and bvint1;1)
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Monosaccharide transporter (MST) family inositol transporter (INT); vacuolar membrane transporter with conserved C-terminal dileucine tonoplast-targeting motif
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS scavenging during cold stress; contributes to freezing/cold tolerance
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed among INT homologues; strongly induced by low temperature, especially in taproot; weaker induction in leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong cold induction in taproot at 4°C/0°C/1°C; highest basal and cold-induced expression in more tolerant genotype GT2
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of functional BvINT1;1 increases inositol accumulation, likely in vacuoles, reduces cold-induced galactinol and especially raffinose accumulation in taproots, and increases ROS-related stress responses under cold
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BvINT1;1 is the only sugar beet tonoplast inositol transporter isoform strongly induced by low temperature, especially in taproots. 2. BvINT1;1 is a highly specific inositol transporter; its loss causes elevated inositol and reduced raffinose accumulation during cold stress. 3. bvint1;1 mutants show stronger ROS-marker expression, impaired photosynthetic performance, and reduced biomass under cold, linking vacuolar inositol export to raffinose-dependent ROS homeostasis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant (W188STOP) analysis; metabolite profiling; vacuole metabolite analysis; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq data mining; subcellular localization in Arabidopsis protoplasts; Xenopus oocyte transport electrophysiology
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (for transient localization assays in Arabidopsis protoplasts)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq expression data from BioProject PRJNA602804
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of BvINT1;1 reduces root biomass even at control temperature and lowers shoot/root biomass and PSII efficiency under cold; increased ROS detoxification demand
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for breeding or engineering improved cold tolerance in sugar beet by enhancing vacuolar inositol export and supporting raffinose-mediated ROS protection
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transport direction is inferred as vacuole-to-cytosol H+ symport in native tonoplast context; mutant allele carries premature stop codon W188STOP and likely produces nonfunctional truncated protein with only five predicted transmembrane helices
